questions.stem: What does them mean?

options.D:
Parents.
I like shopping and talking to people. I think I can learn how to do business.
Maybe selling clothes is a good business for me.
My hobbies are playing computer games and making friends online. We often
share our funny stories by e-mail. I guess I will learn more about computers and
make super-smart computer programs.
My parents have kept lots of pets since I was little. We’ve experienced many
things together, good and bad, happy and sad. Those stories have always stayed
in my mind. One day, I will share them with people by drawing and writing
books.
Though my mom and two sisters are all doctors, I’m sure I will stay as far away
from a hospital as I can. I hate being sick, and I’m afraid of seeing sick people
looking weak and sad. I’ll keep strong by playing my favorite sport, tennis,
every day. One day I’ll join the national team and be another Lu Yen-Hsun.
Lily:
Ryan:
Bill:
Anna:

questions.stem: What can we learn from the preface?

options.A: How Foster started his own restaurant.
options.B: When Foster’s father married Foster’s mother.
options.C: What Foster’s mother taught him about cooking.
options.D:
How Foster learned the art of cooking from his father.
Here is the preface of Nick Foster’s new book Married to Food.
My mother was lousy at cooking. To her, cooking was more like an
exciting experiment. You put some of this and some of that in a pot, and you
wait and see what will happen. “No experiments, no experiences.” is what
she would say when her experiment did not turn out good, and I heard that
a lot.
My father was a good cook, and he loved to cook, too. He often said that
he got my mother to marry him with a table of delicious food, not with a
beautiful ring. “A family needs only one good cook,” he said.
Now I am a cook myself. And I have my own restaurant. I learned how
to cook from my father, of course. From him, I learned the art of cooking.
But I did learn one thing from my mother. It’s her famous saying: “No
experiments, no experiences.”
preface 前言 experiment 實驗
(26-27)

options.B: How water may give a country power.
options.C: How water may play a part in one’s future.
options.D: Why it is hard to get water in poor countries.

questions.stem: What do we know from the reading?

options.A: Children in poor countries die from drinking dirty water every day.
options.B: Girls who spend little time at school have a harder life when they grow up.
options.C: Girls in countries like Tanzania are often paid less for the same job than the boys are.
options.D:
Children from poor families are often kept from school to take care of younger children.
The idea may hit you once or twice a year. You come home on a hot summer
day, hoping to have a cool bath, and find out there is no water. Then you see how
important water is in your everyday life. However, in many parts of the world, water
is not just about one’s everyday needs.
In countries like Tanzania, water is hard to get, and the job of collecting water
falls on women’s shoulders. Girls are often kept home from school to collect water
while their brothers stay at school studying. Studies show Tanzanian girls who live
15 minutes from clean water spend 12% more time at school than those who live an
hour away. More time spent collecting water means less time for learning. For these
girls, “Knowledge is power” is not just words; it is a sad fact in real life. With less
time spent at school, their chances of getting well-paid jobs are small, and they often
have no voice in important matters, like who to marry. These girls are often married
into poor families. They have little money or knowledge to take care of their children,
who often end up dying young. For the baby girls who are lucky enough to live, their
life may still center around “water,” just like it did for their mothers.

questions.explanation: 這題的正確答案是 B。 對應選項內容是「Girls who spend little time at school have a harder life when they grow up.」。 先抓題幹關鍵詞，再用刪去法排除不符合條件的選項。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。

questions.context:
I left you a voice message this morning!
Keith: But you said Matt would go to the bakery! And why did you come here
without me? I was waiting outside your apartment for an hour!
Amber: No! I said I’d have lunch with Matt, and we’d come straight here from the
restaurant. So I asked you to help get the cake after work.
Keith: No, you did not! And why didn’t you answer my calls?
Amber: My cellphone is dead! I said in the message that you could call Matt’s number
if you wanted to find me. Now please tell me you did bring the gift we got for Liz.
Keith: What gift? I’m sure you didn’t say anything about a gift in the message.
Amber: I told you that yesterday!
message 留言
likely 可能
(35-36)

options.B: Those who use their time well will win in the end.
options.C: People are blind to problems that slowly get worse over time.
options.D: It is difficult to give up old habits, but easy to pick up new ones.

questions.explanation: 這題的正確答案是 C。 對應選項內容是「People are blind to problems that slowly get worse over time.」。 先把題幹或前面的資料讀完，再圈出題目真正要比對的資訊。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。

questions.stem: What do we know about Froggie?

options.A: He learned to share what he liked with others.
options.B: He thought what he had hoped for became real.
options.C: He once almost lost a dear friend to a water snake.
options.D:
He wanted to go back to the same pot for a hot bath.
Froggie was a frog who never remembered where he had been.
His biggest dream was to find the best pond to swim freely without
worrying about water snakes. One day, Froggie hopped to a kitchen and
jumped into a pot full of hot water. Froggie felt the burning heat and
hurried out of the water as fast as his legs could carry him. “I almost
died there!” Froggie thought.
A few days later, Froggie went back to the same kitchen, totally
forgetting he had been there. He again hopped into the same pot. This
time, the water inside was cool. He looked around and thought, “There’s
no water snake trying to eat me and I can have this place all to myself!”
At that moment, Froggie knew it was his dream pond. What he didn’t
know was that the water was warming up over a low heat.
After thirty minutes of swimming, Froggie felt the water was
warmer but he thought of this as a nice surprise. “This sure is the
best place for swimming. I get to have a hot bath, too.” During his
comfortable bath, the water kept getting warmer and warmer. Froggie
was so comfortable that he fell asleep. And he never woke up.
(37-40)

questions.explanation: 這題的正確答案是 A。 對應選項內容是「They have serious trouble.」。 先抓題幹關鍵詞，再用刪去法排除不符合條件的選項。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。

questions.stem: What can we learn about the church-goers from the report?

options.A: Between 1980 and 2005, few church-goers were younger than 50.
options.B: In 2010, only 6% of church-goers were younger than 50 years old.
options.C: In 2020, the percentage (%) of church-goers drops to lower than half that in 1980.
options.D: Between 1980 and 2020, the rising number of old church-goers has led to an old church.

questions.explanation: 這題的正確答案是 C。 對應選項內容是「In 2020, the percentage (%) of church-goers drops to lower than half that in 1980.」。 先抓題幹關鍵詞，再用刪去法排除不符合條件的選項。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。

questions.stem: What is talked about in the report?

options.B: How people decide to join a church.
options.C: What happens to churches that are closed.
options.D: Why people have stopped going to church.

questions.explanation: 這題的正確答案是 C。 對應選項內容是「What happens to churches that are closed.」。 先抓題幹關鍵詞，再用刪去法排除不符合條件的選項。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。

questions.stem: Below are four findings from other reports. Which does NOT agree with the idea of the report?

options.A:
“Reports show over 90% of church-goers go to church because their fathers do. In
the last 20 years, 49% of men under 30 have left the church. That means there’s
a 90% chance that their children won’t go to church. This is hard for the church
to take in.”
options.B:
“Between 2003 and 2010, over 1,000 new churches were built in the country.
This may sound like good news. During the same years, however, more than
2,000 were closed.”
options.C:
“In the report, 33% of the people that were interviewed say they never go to
church and another 33% say they used to. Only 15% go to church at least once a
month.”
options.D:
“Of the 15 countries in the report, the percentage (%) of church-goers in some
countries, like Denmark, is lower than 10%; in others, like Poland, the
percentage is higher than 70%.”
試題結束

questions.explanation:
這題的正確答案是 D。 對應選項內容是「“Of the 15 countries in the report, the percentage (%) of church-goers in some
countries, like Denmark, is lower than 10%; in others, like Poland, the
percentage is higher than 70%.”
試題結束」。 先抓題幹關鍵詞，再用刪去法排除不符合條件的選項。 這題來自 104 年會考英語科，建議先回頭確認關鍵概念與題目限制。
